title: Principal Engineer Technical Safety
description:
Job Title: Lead Safety Engineer – Technical Safety Discipline

Job Responsibilities:

  • Report directly to the Section Manager – System Engineering at the basis level and to the Project Engineering Manager at the project level.

  • Serve as the single point of contact for the Safety Engineering Discipline, providing expert support to project teams during Tender, Conceptual, FEED, and Detailed Engineering phases.

  • Deliver cost-effective Safety Engineering design solutions and manage all technical aspects related to Safety Engineering throughout the project lifecycle, ensuring compliance with statutory, company, and client requirements.

  • Implement company work processes, procedures, and quality policies as mandated by the project.

  • Maintain thorough knowledge of company contractual requirements and scope boundaries, supporting the project through effective management of change within assigned areas.

  • Manage resources and progress for Safety Engineering scopes, ensuring internal and external deliverables align with project budget and schedule.

  • Guarantee the technical integrity of all Safety Engineering design activities and deliverables under supervision, ensuring adherence to project performance, integrity, quality, and HSE standards.

  • Review and contribute technical inputs related to equipment/package specifications, bid inquiries, bid evaluations, and procurement for assigned safety equipment/packages.

  • Manage technical safety interfaces with equipment and package suppliers to ensure compliance with client’s Basis of Design, standards, and project specifications.

  • Supervise or act as the package responsible engineer for safety, life-saving, and firefighting equipment.

  • Identify, assess, and implement preventive measures for all risks related to assigned areas in a timely manner.

  • Support verification activities required by verification bodies, classification societies, or other approving authorities.

  • Represent the Technical Safety discipline in meetings, audits, verifications, workshops, and technical reviews, ensuring timely resolution of identified issues.

  • Provide technical safety support at fabrication sites as needed.

  • Continuously improve working methods, tools, and procedures while contributing to the development and experience transfer within the Technical Safety discipline.

  • Support the Discipline Head in workload distribution, mentoring, training, development of direct reports, and performance appraisals.

  • Perform additional duties as assigned by supervisors.

Work Experience Requirements:

  • Minimum 15 years of experience in multidisciplinary engineering environments within upstream oil & gas projects (fixed and floating installations).

  • At least 4 years in a Lead Safety Engineer role.

  • Experience with medium to large brownfield and/or greenfield upstream oil and gas projects, with comprehensive knowledge of project phases, milestones, deliverables, and interfaces.

  • Extensive experience developing and implementing Safety Design Philosophies and Specifications for Escape and Emergency Response, Active and Passive Fire Protection, Fire and Gas detection, Accidental Load Design, Emergency Shutdown, Hazardous Area classification, and Human Factor Engineering.

  • Proven expertise in creating and reviewing Safety Engineering deliverables such as Escape Route and Safety Equipment Layouts, Firewater Calculations, P&IDs, Firewater Hydraulics, Fire and Gas Drawings, Hazardous Area and Noise Specifications, and Flare Radiation assessments.

  • Experience specifying, managing, and implementing third-party safety study recommendations, including HAZID/ENVID, SIL classifications, smoke and gas dispersion modeling, Fire and Explosion Risk Analyses, Evacuation Analyses, QRA, ALARP assessments, Fire and Gas Mapping, and Noise Studies.

  • Thorough knowledge of industry standards and codes including API, ISO, NORSOK, NFPA, EU Directives, and other relevant Technical Safety standards.

  • Proficiency in business software (MS Office) and technical safety-related software such as Pipenet, DNV PHAST, Flaresim, or similar.

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical, Chemical, Safety Engineering, or equivalent engineering discipline.

Other Skills:

  • Excellent technical communication skills (verbal and written) with the ability to effectively present technical issues to design teams, clients, regulatory authorities, and verification bodies.

  • Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to collaborate at all organizational levels.

  • Self-motivated with the capability to work independently, make sound decisions, analyze problems, and provide targeted solutions.
